title: What Lies in the Woods
author: Kate Alice Marshall
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "What Lies in the Woods" book by Kate Alice Marshall?
Options:
A. A decade less than my present age. Did Stahl transport her somewhere? Had he pulled her into the woods, concealed her where no one could locate her? I trembled. I understood now what Cass intended, saying she wanted to inform Persephone she'd had a daughter. He's gone, I wanted to whisper to those bones. I'd discovered Persephone, just as Liv did, and this must be how Liv felt, too, like she had explored the underworld for her spirit and at last caught sight of her. Jessi wasn't Persephone but Eurydice, and Liv was Orpheus, guiding her upward only to recklessly, inevitably look back though prohibited from doing so, and now both were lost below.
B. Ten years younger than my current age. Had Stahl given her a ride somewhere? Had he taken her into the forest against her will, hiding her where she could not be found? I shivered. Now I grasped what Cass meant about wanting to tell Persephone she had a daughter. He's passed away, I wished to murmur to those bones. I had found Persephone, just like Liv did, and this must be how Liv felt too, as if she had searched the underworld for her ghost and finally saw her. Jessi was not Persephone but Eurydice, and Liv was Orpheus, leading her back up but foolishly, inevitably glancing back though forbidden to do so, and now both were lost below.  
C. Ten years junior to my current age. Had Stahl given her a lift? Had he taken her into the forest, hidden her where nobody could find her? I shivered. I grasped now what Cass meant, saying she wanted to tell Persephone she'd had a daughter. He's deceased, I wished to murmur to those bones. I'd located Persephone, just like Liv did, and this must be how Liv felt too, as if she had searched the underworld for her ghost and finally glimpsed her. Jessi wasn't Persephone but Eurydice, and Liv was Orpheus, leading her back toward the surface only to foolishly, inevitably glance back despite being forbidden to do so, and now both were lost below.
D. A decade younger than I was now. Had Stahl offered her a ride? Had he dragged her into the woods, hidden her away where no one could find her? I shuddered. I understood what Cass meant, now, when she said she wanted to tell Persephone that shed had a daughter. Hes dead, I wanted to whisper to those bones. Id found Persephone, just as Liv had, and this must be how Liv had felt, too, like she had searched the underworld for her ghost and sighted her at last. Jessi wasnt Persephone but Eurydice, and Liv was Orpheus, guiding her back toward the surface only tofoolishly, inevitablylook back as she had been forbidden to do, and now both of them were lost below.
Answer:

I understood now what Cass intended, saying she wanted to inform Persephone she'd had a daughter. He's gone, I wanted to whisper to those bones. I'd discovered Persephone, just as Liv did, and this must be how Liv felt, too, like she had explored the underworld for her spirit and at last caught sight of her. Jessi wasn't Persephone but Eurydice, and Liv was Orpheus, guiding her upward only to recklessly, inevitably look back though prohibited from doing so, and now both were lost below.
